policies.name: TS-iPASS / TG-iPASS (Telangana Industrial Project Approval and Self-Certification System)
policies.what_companies_get: All industrial approvals in under 15 days via single window; self-certification allows construction commencement from Day 1; TS-iPASS Act PDF on policies page. Portal rebranded TG-iPASS under the new government.
policies.application_status: Portal live - ipass.telangana.gov.in returns HTTP 200 with full TG-iPASS application site
policies.site_notes: Homepage is a 22MB page titled TG-iPASS (18 mentions of TG-iPASS vs 17 legacy TS-iPASS). Act PDF: invest.telangana.gov.in/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/TS-iPASS-BILL.pdf

policies.name: T-IDEA (Telangana State Industrial Development and Entrepreneur Advancement) incentive scheme
policies.incentive_type: Capital subsidy, tax reimbursement, power tariff, land
policies.what_companies_get: Large industries: reimbursement of 50% net VAT/CST or SGST for 7 years from commencement OR until 100% fixed capital recovery, whichever first; fixed power cost reimbursement @ Re 1/unit for 5 years from CoCP; 25% rebate on land cost in industrial estates (capped ~Rs 10 lakh); 50% of infrastructure cost for standalone units capped Rs 1 crore (roads/power/water); 25% subsidy on cleaner production measures capped Rs 5 lakh; 100% stamp/transfer duty reimbursement (as cross-referenced in Life Sciences Policy 2026).
policies.status: Active (still the base industrial incentive framework; Life Sciences Policy 2026-30 explicitly grants large-unit SGST 'as per T-IDEA')
policies.application_status: Incentive applications routed through TG-iPASS ecosystem; portal live
policies.site_notes: Scheme PDF also listed: invest.telangana.gov.in/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/T-IDEA-Telangana-State-Industrial-Development-and-Entrepreneur-Advancement.pdf

policies.name: T-PRIDE (Telangana State Program for Rapid Incubation of Dalit Entrepreneurs)
policies.what_companies_get: Enhanced version of T-IDEA incentives for SC/ST-owned enterprises (document verified present; percentages not extracted in this pass)
policies.application_status: Via TG-iPASS; portal live
policies.site_notes: PDF listed on official policies page; incentive numbers not extracted from the PDF in this session

policies.name: Telangana Next-Gen Life Sciences Policy 2026-30 (covers Genome Valley / Green Pharma City ecosystem)
policies.incentive_type: Capital subsidy, SGST reimbursement, power subsidy, employment and skilling grants
policies.what_companies_get: 100% stamp+transfer duty reimbursement; power reimbursement Rs 1.00/unit for 5 years from CoCP; 25% investment subsidy on FCI max Rs 30 lakh (micro/small) + extra 20% max Rs 20 lakh for women-owned; 25% land cost reimbursement max Rs 10 lakh in TGIIC/approved private parks; net SGST reimbursement 100%/5yrs (micro-small), 75%/7yrs (medium), 50%/7yrs (large, per T-IDEA), all capped at 100% FCI; CSR matching grant 10% of project cost max Rs 1 crore; EPF reimbursement Rs 2,000/domiciled employee up to 500 employees (twice in policy term); 50% skill-training cost max Rs 2,000/person; 50% quality/ZED certification subsidy max Rs 2 lakh; 25% cleaner production subsidy max Rs 5 lakh; 25% land conversion charges max Rs 10 lakh (MSME); 30% international marketing cost max Rs 2 lakh/yr; 25% or Rs 4 lakh grant for licensing patented tech. Additional negotiated incentives for focus industries decided case-by-case by Telangana Life Sciences Foundation.
policies.status: Active - NEW policy issued under current government (uploaded Jan 2026, policy period 2026-2030)
policies.application_status: Incentives administered via TLSF/TG-iPASS; dedicated Genome Valley and Green Pharma City pages live
policies.site_notes: Full incentive schedule extracted from the official PDF (6.2MB). Genome Valley: lifesciences.telangana.gov.in/genome-valley/, Green Pharma City: /green-pharma-city/

Old Industrial Framework 2014 and T-IDEA remain the operative incentive regime; the Next-Gen Life Sciences Policy 2026-30 is the first major new sector policy verified

policies.name: AP Industrial Development Policy 4.0 (2024-29)
policies.incentive_type: Capital subsidy, SGST reimbursement, employment subsidy, PLI top-up, stamp duty, decarbonization subsidy
policies.what_companies_get: Four bands: Sub-Large (FCI Rs 51-500cr, 2yr build), Large (501-1,000cr, 3yr), Mega (1,001-5,000cr, 3yr), Ultra Mega (5,001cr+, 4yr); total incentives capped at 100% of FCI. Sub-Large: 12% FCI capital subsidy (5 annual installments; -0.5%/month for delay; +5% extra for women/BC/SC/ST/specially-abled AP-domiciled owners); power reimbursement Rs 1/unit for 2 years; 100% net SGST reimbursement 5 years; 1% of annual export turnover reimbursed 3 years (local procurement). Large: 12% FCI (7 installments, milestone-linked 6%+6%); 100% net SGST 5 years. Mega/Ultra Mega: 15% FCI (10 installments, 5%+5%+5% by capacity milestones) plus tailor-made packages case-by-case. All bands: PLI top-up = 10% of GoI PLI incentive sanctioned, capped 5% of FCI; employment creation subsidy 8-10% of FCI by employment-to-investment ratio (E/I>=5 gets 10%), paid over 5-10 years; decarbonization capital subsidy 10-35% of eligible cost capped 6% FCI; 100% stamp duty + transfer duty reimbursement (one-time); 100% land conversion fee reimbursement. Early Bird Scheme: first 200 projects getting CFE within 18 months of notification get 30% of FCI investment subsidy; PLI-sector value-added manufacturing gets 40% of FCI (CFE window 24 months). ESCROW-based disbursement mechanism being set up.
policies.application_status: Single Desk Portal: >93 clearances from 19 departments in 21 days, end-to-end online with payment and tracking; login portal linked from apindustries.gov.in homepage (link verified present, login page itself not tested)
policies.site_notes: Full 32-page official PDF downloaded from apindustries.gov.in and text-extracted; numbers above are verbatim from Sections 8-9

policies.what_companies_get: 93+ regulatory clearances across 19 departments within 21 days; three-tier self-certification/deemed-approval regime; dedicated Investment Facilitation Cell with a liaison officer per investor; country-specific FDI desks
policies.status: Active (SDP launched 2015, being upgraded to Single Window 2.0 under IDP 4.0)
policies.application_status: Login portal linked from apindustries.gov.in homepage (Introduction.aspx / LoginPortal); dedicated apsingledesk.ap.gov.in domain did not resolve

policies.name: Kerala Industrial Policy 2023 incentive package (administered via K-RIIS)
policies.incentive_type: Capital subsidy, SGST reimbursement, electricity duty exemption, employment incentive, ESG/IR4.0 support
policies.what_companies_get: 18 incentive categories: MSME capital subsidy up to 45% (caps Rs 40-200 lakh by category); large/mega investment subsidy 10% of FCI max Rs 10 crore in phases; 100% SGST reimbursement on capital investment for 5 years; 100% electricity duty exemption 5 years; employment enhancer up to Rs 5,000/month per employee for 1 year; stamp duty waiver up to 100% for manufacturing in industrial parks; 4% interest loans up to Rs 10 lakh; trade fair reimbursement up to 100% max Rs 5 lakh/yr; IP creation 50% max Rs 30 lakh; quality certification 50% max Rs 25 lakh/yr; sustainability (ETP/renewables) 25% max Rs 25 lakh; IR 4.0 adoption 20% max Rs 25 lakh; R&D with universities 20% max Rs 1 crore; startup scale-up loans to Rs 1 crore; PLI investor top-up 20% of fixed investment max Rs 1 crore
policies.application_status: Applications live via K-RIIS portal (kriis.kerala.gov.in) per KSIDC; helpline 1800-890-1030
policies.site_notes: Policy page also at ksidc.org/invest-kerala/industrial-policy-2023/ and industry.kerala.gov.in/index.php/industrial-policy; full policy PDF at invest.kerala.gov.in/media/urulj14k/industrial-policy.pdf

policies.name: Entrepreneur Support Scheme (ESS)
policies.what_companies_get: 15% of capital investment max Rs 30 lakh (general); 25% max Rs 40 lakh for young (18-45), women, SC/ST and NRK entrepreneurs; additional 10% (max Rs 10 lakh each) for priority sectors (rubber, agro-processing, biotech, pharma etc.), for units in Idukki/Wayanad/Kasargode/Pathanamthitta, and for new technology from approved research institutions; overall ceiling Rs 40 lakh; three stages - startup support (50% of eligible, max Rs 3 lakh, pre-production on bank loan sanction), investment support (post-production, no bank loan needed), technology support (within 6 months of tech adoption)
policies.application_status: Online application portal live at ess.kerala.gov.in/login (application fee Rs 1,105); processed by district committees chaired by District Collector
policies.site_notes: Also listed on industry.kerala.gov.in schemes menu

policies.name: Campus Industrial Parks (CIP) Scheme
policies.incentive_type: Infrastructure grant for campus-based industrial parks
policies.what_companies_get: Educational institutions (colleges/polytechnics/ITIs) with min 5 acres (2 acres for a Standard Design Factory) get up to Rs 1.5 crore infrastructure support @ Rs 20 lakh/acre (roads, power, drainage, labs) plus an additional Rs 1.5 crore for Standard Design Factory setup; reimbursement basis; 14 eligible manufacturing/service sectors
policies.status: Active (state targeted 25 CIPs in 2024 per invest-kerala news)
policies.application_status: Apply via Directorate of Industries & Commerce web portal with Higher Education Dept NOC; district Site Selection Committee then state apex committee issue the developer permit
policies.site_notes: Cross-listed at industry.kerala.gov.in/index.php/schemes-mainmenu/campus-industrial-park

policies.name: Private Industrial Estate (PIE) Scheme (private industrial-park policy)
policies.incentive_type: Infrastructure subsidy for private park developers
policies.what_companies_get: Up to Rs 30 lakh/acre, max Rs 3 crore per estate, for power/water/roads/common facilities; min 10 acres (5 acres for Special Development Facilities); land owned or leased 30 years; PIE Developer Permit includes single-window clearances; up to 30% of land may go to service sector (no retail). Policy revised to allow individual developers per invest-kerala news
policies.application_status: Structured evaluation by Director of Industries & Commerce; final approval by State Level Selection Committee
policies.site_notes: Tenant-company incentives not spelled out on the page (they use standard IP-2023/ESS incentives)

policies.name: K-SWIFT single window clearance
policies.what_companies_get: Kerala Single Window Interface for Fast & Transparent clearance - composite application for licences/clearances for industrial units
policies.application_status: Portal live at kswift.kerala.gov.in (HTTP 200, title 'K-Swift')
policies.site_notes: Portal responds and serves the application interface

policies.name: KSIDC direct loan schemes

TELANGANA: 'Meet or Beat' tailored-incentive guarantee for projects >USD 30M or >1,000 jobs

TELANGANA: MSME Policy 2024 booklet; Data Centres Policy; 2nd ICT Policy 2021; IoT Policy 2017; Gaming & Animation Policy 2016; Cyber Security Policy; Data Analytics Policy; Solar Policy; Logistics Policy 2021; Food Processing Policy 2021; Textile policy (T-TAP); Rural Technology Centres Policy; separate 'Incentives for Expansion of IT/ITeS Companies' document

ANDHRA PRADESH: AP MSME & Entrepreneur Development Policy 4.0 (2024-29) with 2025 amendments (GO Ms 22, GO Ms 110); Early Bird Project Scheme (30%/40% of FCI investment subsidy overriding band rates); AP Private Industrial Parks Policy 4.0; de-carbonization subsidy (10-35% of eligible cost); employment-creation subsidy tied to E/I ratio; ESCROW-based incentive disbursement mechanism

KERALA: K-RIIS incentive-application portal (kriis.kerala.gov.in); MSME Scale-Up Mission 1000; Interest Subvention Scheme for nano units; Margin Money Grant to nano units; One Family One Enterprise (OFOE); One Local Body One Product (OLOP); ASHA (handicrafts artisans) scheme; MSME Insurance Scheme; Kerala Entrepreneur Loan Scheme (KELS); stressed-MSME revival & rehabilitation schemes; KSIDC loan products; draft Kerala Logistics Parks Policy 2024; draft Export Promotion Policy; Bio360 Life Sciences Park and KSIDC industrial parks
